title: Migration Page Layout Overhaul and Column Updates
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want the migration page to have an organized layout with clear navigation between Dictionary and Migration views, so that I can efficiently manage object data without visual clutter.
description:
Comprehensive UX overhaul of the migrate.php page to improve layout for demos and daily use.

## Layout Changes
- Move the object selection / breadcrumb control to right-aligned
- Move the Dictionary / Migration menu above the current breadcrumb location
- Make the static "Migration" label dynamic based on the selected view and object (e.g., "Dictionary: Account (Account)")
- Use light navigation styling instead of blue selection color

## Dictionary View Columns
Reporting Field | SFDC Field Label | Definition | Metric Relation | SFDC Field API Name | Data Type

## Filter Improvements
- "Filter by Migrate" hidden when on Dictionary tab
- New "Filter by Reporting Field" filter hidden on Migration tab
- Change dropdown default from "Select Option" to "All"

## Styling
- Adjust column spacing so picklist values don't consume excessive horizontal space
- Load FontAwesome icons on all pages
acceptance_criteria: Dictionary/Migration menu appears above the breadcrumb area
Page label dynamically reflects the selected view and object name
Dictionary view shows all six required columns
Filter by Migrate is hidden on Dictionary tab
Filter by Reporting Field is hidden on Migration tab
Dropdown defaults display 'All' instead of 'Select Option'
Column spacing provides reasonable distribution across the table
FontAwesome is loaded on all pages
ux_changes: Major layout restructuring of migrate.php including dynamic labels, right-aligned breadcrumbs, view-specific filters, and improved column spacing across Dictionary and Migration views.
implementation_notes: Dynamic label format: '[View]: [Label] ([Object])'. View-specific filter visibility controlled via JavaScript based on active tab selection.
